records.ety: [Prob. of Scand. origin, and akin to glad, a.; cf. also W. golead, goleuad, a lighting, illumination, fr. goleu light, clear, bright, goleu fwlch glade, lit., a light or clear defile.]

defs: An open passage through a wood; a grassy open or cleared space in a forest.
quotes: There interspersed in lawns and opening glades.

defs: An opening in the ice of rivers or lakes, or a place left unfrozen; also, smooth ice.
